evidence.quote: Late cardiotoxicity related to HER2-targeted cancer therapy
We include seventeen patients with metastatic disease who developed CTRCD after at least five years of continuous anti-HER2 therapy.
data about the incidence and risk factors for developing late Cancer therapy-related cardiac dysfunction (CTRCD) are missing.
normalized their left ventricular ejection fraction at a follow-up.

initial_request_score.fix_suggestion: No changes needed; extraction accurately reflects the paper's abstract and introduction.

evidence.quote: referred to the Cardio-Oncology service at Royal Brompton Hospital from 2011 to 2023.
We identified seventeen patients, referred to our clinic between 2018 and 2023, fulfilling our inclusion criteria.
All patients had a normal baseline echocardiogram with a mean LVEF of 63.7±6.0%.
Our cohort included sixteen women (94%) with metastatic HER2+ breast cancer and one man with metastatic HER2+ gastric adenocarcinoma.

Nine patients (53%) were diagnosed with asymptomatic mild CTRCD, 5 patients (29%) with asymptomatic moderate CTRCD, 1 patient (6%) with symptomatic moderate CTRCD and 2 patients (12%) with symptomatic severe CTRCD.

Events occurred after a median time of 6.5 years... lowest (median) LVEF... 49%... Most (16/17)... normalized their left ventricular ejection fraction
Our study has demonstrated that CTRCD can occur after many years of stability on anti-HER2 therapy and reinforces the importance of continuing cardiovascular surveillance in this population.
